title: Apple’s New Siri AI Is Now Gemini
summary: Apple revealed a rebuilt Siri with personal context, screen awareness, and Visual Intelligence at WWDC 2026, powered by Google Gemini. Apple shares fell after the keynote and the EU will not get the feature at launch. The video questions whether Apple is catching up in AI or packaging rival technology within its own ecosystem.
writeup: Apple's WWDC 2026 unveiled a rebuilt Siri AI, repositioned as a personal context layer with screen awareness, cross-device conversation history, and Write with Siri composing text in a user's own stylistic register. The market reaction was harsh: Apple lost more than $200 billion from its intraday high, not from the prior close, driven mainly by Reuters and Business Insider reports that the new Siri is built partly on Google Gemini models running on Nvidia cloud infrastructure. The through-line is Apple's core tension: a privacy-first brand now leaning on a rival's intelligence stack to close a two-year AI gap, while key markets (EU and China) are excluded at launch.
key_points: Apple rebuilt Siri from the ground up with personal context access, reading mail, messages, photos, calendar, contacts, and files on device to answer questions no general chatbot could answer without manual input.

Screen awareness lets Siri read what is currently displayed and act on it, with demonstrated tasks including brainstorming potluck dishes from a message and entering a concert ticket lottery directly from a Siri prompt.
Write with Siri adapts drafting style per contact: if a user normally sends a manager terse bullet points, Siri drafts in that register; if messages to a friend are casual, Siri mirrors that tone.

Reuters reported that some Apple foundation models were built using Google Gemini technology, with larger models running on Nvidia cloud infrastructure, contradicting Apple's years-long privacy-first independent AI narrative and triggering the investor selloff.
The accurate market loss figure is roughly $230 billion erased from Apple's intraday peak to close ($317 to $301.54), not from the prior day's close, where the actual drop was closer to $85 billion.
Siri AI is absent from EU and China at launch due to Digital Markets Act concerns and local regulation, and is currently limited to developer testing in English on supported newer devices.
